title: Fantasy FIFA World Cup 2026 team previews: Norway
original_text:
World Cup Live. FPL Coming Soon. Lowest Price This Year

The FFScout Podcast is a daily FPL podcast providing you the tips you need to succeed. Featuring FPL Harry, Joe, Neale, Sam, FPL General and more!

Win prizes and try to beat the FFScout community!

In our next team preview for the Fantasy FIFA World Cup 2026 game , we turn our attention to Norway.

With names like Erling Haaland ($10.5m) and Martin Odegaard ($7.7m) in their squad, Stale Solbakken’s men could be a force to be reckoned with this summer.

In these country-by-country guides, we’ll be looking at the best players from each nation, reviewing the road to the World Cup and more.

Norway cruised through their qualification campaign, with eight wins from eight – a perfect record.

That run included surprisingly comfortable home and away victories over Italy, 3-0 in Oslo and 4-1 at the San Siro.

No team came close to matching the Norwegians from an attacking perspective.

They scored 37 times, eight more than any other team in European qualification. They also finished third for non-penalty expected goals (xG) and xG conceded per 90 minutes:

Haaland netted 16 (!) times, the most by any player from any continent in qualifying.

Remarkably, none of his strikes were from the penalty spot.

He averaged a goal every 46 minutes, and naturally led the way among teammates for NPxG (7.61) and shots (40).

Haaland wasn’t Norway’s only attacking threat, though.

Alexander Sorloth ($6.8m) netted five goals, while Thelo Aasgaard ($5.5m) contributed four – the latter scored all four against Moldova.

Odegaard enjoyed a successful qualifying campaign, even if he wasn’t always fully fit.

The playmaker recorded six assists in five matches.

Helped by his role on set plays, he also racked up 21 key passes, the most of any Norway player.

Norway’s dynamic right-back, Julian Ryerson ($4.2m), chipped in with four assists, which is why he is being considered by so many Fantasy managers this summer. He’s another who is on corner-taking duty.

Norway defeated Sweden 3-1 in their first World Cup warm-up match, even without their two best players, Haaland and Odegaard.

Jorgen Strand Larsen ($5.6m) netted twice, while winger Antonio Nusa ($6.1m) found the net with one of his six attempts.

Corner-taker Ryerson also stood out with two assists and eight (!) key passes.

Then, on Sunday, Odegaard netted a late equaliser to force a 1-1 draw with Morocco.

Orjan Nyland ($4.2m) started in goal, while David Moller Wolfe ($4.0m) appears to be first-choice at left-back with another start here.

Nusa played 45 minutes before being replaced by Andreas Schjelderup ($6.2m), while Haaland completed 72 minutes, getting him up to speed before the start of the tournament.

Ryerson was as creative as ever, with four key passes.

The fixture order has been kind to Norway, with an opener against Iraq, who are 57th in the FIFA World Rankings.

A potentially tricky but winnable match against Senegal follows, before encountering European heavyweights France in Round 3.

By then, Norway will be hoping to have qualification to the knockout phase wrapped up.

For Fantasy managers, the Round 1 fixture against Iraq is clearly the one to focus on. Following that, and certainly by Round 3, it should be safe to move their assets on.

Erling Haaland ($10.5m) will be in many Fantasy teams this summer, and for good reason.

On penalties with a huge open-play threat, a Round 1 match with Iraq carries real haul potential.

However, it gets trickier thereafter, which is why Haaland is considered a solid candidate for the 12th Man chip in Round 1.

The primary drawback to this approach is that you can’t captain him against Iraq, so an alternative approach is to start with Haaland and then move him on for Kylian Mbappe ($10.5m) or Harry Kane ($10.5m).

Martin Odegaard ($7.7m) currently qualifies for the Scouting Bonus, although it is tight, with 4.6% ownership.

Should the Arsenal schemer remain under the threshold, he could be a superb differential option.

He provided six assists in qualifying, and given that Norway are likely to dominate possession against Iraq, his ability to deliver pinpoint through balls to Haaland could be key.

He could be in the mix for the chances created/direct free-kick bonuses, too.

Norway sit third on the clean sheet odds list for Round 1 , only behind Germany and Spain.

Full-back Julian Ryerson ($4.2m) will provide width down the right flank, having supplied 18 assists in 42 matches for Borussia Dortmund last season.

Helped by his role at set plays, he was also the top defender for expected assists (xA) in European qualification.

However, at 7.7% ownership, Ryerson won’t bank Scouting Bonus points.

If you do want to target those extra points, consider goalkeeper Orjan Nyland ($4.2m), centre-back Torbjorn Heggem ($3.7m) or David Moller Wolfe ($4.0m) from the Norway backline.

All three of those cheap options were a mainstay in qualifying, starting all eight matches.

My most likely final draft(unless something happens) Rochet-Vargas DeCuyper-Tah-Elvedi-Cucurella-N.Mendes Wirtz-Sane-Bruno-Sabitzer-J.Rodriguez Oyarzabal-Mbappe-Nunez Haaland 12th man. Sane and Nunez only players I'm not 100% on atm. Yildiz and Embolo can suddenly take their places.

Sane a good bet with all the injuries they have for the RW spot. Nunez I'm not so sure about, he's been pretty poor this season.

Here's my draft. Any ideas? Rangel Crepeau Kimmich Cucu Mendes Ryerson Munoz Wirtz Olise Bruno Diaz Xhaka Mbappe Oyarza Havertz 0.7m itb, so I can do Wirtz to Raphinha in MD2

I have 7 of those. Dont you want to captain Haaland wk 1? I'm gambling with no Mbappe in wk 1

I think I have enough alternative captains, so it's a risk I'm willing to take.

Looks good tbf. As you say yourself, multiple C options if needed.

Enjoy! Which match day do you prefer to be used by thousands?

Not really looked at. Don’t think I’m going to bother doing a team.

Exactly what Norway needs, rain. Also Nusa will start this match, so probably helps alot.

Just as a reminder: Scouting bonus only kicks in if the player has scored 4 or more points in hus match. Also the scouting bonus only activates if the player is on less than 5% ownership at the kick off if their respective match. https://www.fantasyfootballscout.co.uk/2026/06/05/fantasy-fifa-world-cup-2026-what-is-scouting-bonus

Wonder if Fifa servers will handle the calculations....

"kick off of their respective match" It was fun while it lasted.

Changes very little. If a player scores less than 4 points he's probably getting yeeted out anyway.

Too risky with Olmo + Torres 12th man? Vargas Crepeau Kimmich Olivera Widmer Wolfe Ordonez Olise Bruno Olmo Wirtz Sane Haaland Mbappe Oyarzabal + Torres 12th

Don't like your keepers personally. Prefer Rangel, Galindez, Rochet rather than those guys. Better fixtures for the first two games. It's pretty good overall though. I like this team. Would save the 12th man though rather than throw it on Torres r1. There are juicier games in 2 imo. France vs Iraq and Brazil vs Haiti are both better games to target and we'd have the benefit of having seen all the teams play in MD1. I also wouldn't double up on any single team so you have more captaincy options. Going Messi over Mbappe for instance gives you more options.

I don't agree with your GK's, but I like your other points. Thanks!

I have £4.3m in the bank and no logical way to use it.

fiveguys for breakfast, lunch and dinner for next 196 years?

Have you ever been to 5 Guys? Would only last you about 2 weeks that

So many good midfielders - Olise, Bruno, Wirtz, etc. And I really think a reliable premium forward line is good - Oyarzabal, Mbappe Kane etc. Surely at least one upgrade? Surprising to have 4.3, especially as there's not really a bench

What is the point of the Vice Captain if we can change captain every day? It's possible to have 7 x different captains in one game week so when would the vice captain every come into play?!

It only activates if you do not change your captain on a round and that captain does not play at all in a match. As soon as you change your main captain it becomes obsolete.

Heard somebody say that there was a glitch in the Club World Cup version of this game whereby you couldn’t switch a player from VC to C after the deadline. Probs best not to have your VC on anyone you’re potentially going to (C) just in case they haven’t fixed it.

If you select a vice as a captain in the same match and the captain doesn't play, the vice is then captain. Also for the final

One thing that's underutilized in this game is using the fixture order to your advantage. Not every team is created equal under these rules. Colombia might have great fixtures and good value, but they play on the last day. It means that if they don't score well you'll have no one else to come in. It's better to build a squad that has expensive late game options that you will find a spot for and would be okay anchoring the team. Players like Bruno Fernandes and Messi. And take your punts, especially on defense, early. It's why a team like Switzerland or Mexico are better than Colombia or Uruguay for cheap defender punts imo. Because you can roll them out early and replace if they don't keep a cleanie. Your team should be structured like a pyramid - lots of cheaper, differential risks in the first few days, and fewer but top end captaincy picks for the last few days.

thanks. now i have to redraft the whole team

My first draft, my only draft: Kobel Cucrella Nuno Mendes Hakimi De Ketelaene Kubo Bruno Yamal Kane(c) Mbappe Alvarez El Shenawy / Sarr / R.Rodriguez / Ordóñez

CDK is a lock for me.. Listed out of position and potentially on pens.. under 5% Am I missing something?

0% chance he's on pens with KdB + Lukaku in the side. Plus his domestic record is pretty dreadful. 3 goals in 31 appearances is awful.

I think he starts over Lukaku, at least for the first game. He took Belgiums last penalty with KDB on the pitch, but KDB did take the one previously.

He's scored 4 in 5 for Belgium, and took the last penalty (v USA) when KDB was on the pitch. How is that 0%?

Had FPL Harry released his MD1 draft yet anyone??

Which nation could surprise out of these the first round? Different game, but set-up is quite similar to FFS but worse countries get an extra factor. Factor 3 Tsjechië, Tunesië, Schotland, Congo, Oezbekistan, Qatar, Irak en Zuid-Afrika 4 Saudi-Arabië, Jordanië, Bosnië, Kaapverdië, Ghana, Curaçao, Haïti en Nieuw-Zeeland Need one more defender (now Ryerson (factor 2), Dedic, Robertson? and midfielder for my game - currently have Soucek, McTominay, (Kimmich is factor 1) Attack Haaland (factor 2), Dzeko (c), Semenyo, Taremi Any advice welcome, thanks!

Team: Rangel crepeau Kimmich Doue sanchez widmer Mendez Olise Sane De Ketelare Bruno Raphina Haaland Mbappe Oyarzable Bit worried about lack of Spain. Any tips or help appreciated. Cheers

In other news...McKenna stepps down from Ipswich job.

Cab you roll transfers in this game folks??

"During the group stage, one transfer can be carried over into the next group stage Round. However, due to the unlimited transfer limit between the last game of Round 3 and the start of the Round of 32 you will not be able to carry a transfer over at this stage." So yes, but only 1 and only in the group stage - so useless assuming a WC during the groups.
